answer: The SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA database is read-only: no DDL or DML can be performed, and no cloning or Time Travel is allowed, whereas user-created databases allow these operations. The one exception is that you can use all the same commands and syntax to view and query the sample database as you would with a user-created database.

A user would need to execute the SQL statements if the sample database has not been created for their account and they want access to it. The two steps they must complete are: 1) Create the database from the share using `CREATE DATABASE SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA FROM SHARE SFC_SAMPLES.SAMPLE_DATA;` and 2) Grant the PUBLIC role access to the database using `GRANT IMPORTED PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA TO ROLE PUBLIC;`.
2; referencing the fully-qualified name of the table in the query, or specifying the sample database (and schema) for the session using USE DATABASE and/or USE SCHEMA commands
The retention time for the SNOWFLAKE_SAMPLE_DATA database is 1, and no Time Travel can be performed on the database or any schemas/tables in the database.
